architecture.summary: A two-stage reasoning framework for robotic anomaly detection and reactive planning. Stage 1: a fast binary anomaly classifier operating in LLM embedding space, which may trigger Stage 2: a slower generative LLM fallback selection stage. Both stages feed into a model predictive control (MPC) strategy that maintains feasibility of multiple fallback plans to account for the slow reasoner's latency, ensuring safety during anomaly response.
trained_components: fast binary anomaly classifier (in LLM embedding space)

biblio.title: Real-Time Anomaly Detection and Reactive Planning with Large Language Models

baseline.name: autoregressive reasoning with state-of-the-art GPT models

claims.statement: Fast anomaly classifier outperforms autoregressive reasoning with state-of-the-art GPT models, even when instantiated with relatively small language models

Two-stage framework with MPC strategy maintains joint feasibility of continuing along various fallback plans to account for slow reasoner's latency as soon as an anomaly is detected, thus ensuring safety
Runtime monitor improves the trustworthiness of dynamic robotic systems, such as quadrotors or autonomous vehicles, under resource and time constraints
Fast binary anomaly classifier mitigates computational expense of LLMs such that they may be applied online to robotic systems
